basic_details.job_title: WBMSC Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 41 Posts
basic_details.short_information: West Bengal Municipal Service Commission (WBMSC) has officially released a notification for the recruitment of 41 Medical Officer (General) Posts. This recruitment drive offers a great opportunity for candidates looking for healthcare jobs. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the official website.

application_fee.general_fee: Rs. 200 (Rs. 150 application fee + Rs. 50 processing charges)

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 37 years Age Relaxation: SC/ST: 5 years OBC: 3 years PWD: up to 45 years Ex-Serviceman: period of actual military service + 3 years KMC Contractual: up to 3 years

qualification.education_qualification: MBBS degree from a recognized University and duly registered in Medical Council. Preference will be given to candidates possessing postgraduate qualification like DPH/MD (Community Medicine)/ MD (PSM) from a recognized university. 01 year experience in practice of medicine/working experience in the field of Public Health.
qualification.details: Medical Officer (General): MBBS degree from a recognized University and duly registered in Medical Council. Preference will be given to candidates possessing postgraduate qualification like DPH/MD (Community Medicine)/ MD (PSM) from a recognized university. 01 year experience in practice of medicine/working experience in the field of Public Health.

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the WBMSC Medical Officer (General) Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 11-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of WBMSC to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the WBMSC Medical Officer (General) recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date for registration and submission of the online application is 11 May 2026. Candidates are advised to apply well in advance to avoid any last-minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Medical Officer (General) post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an MBBS degree from a recognized University and be duly registered with the Medical Council. Preference will be given to those with postgraduate qualifications like DPH/MD (Community Medicine)/ MD (PSM).

faq.question: Q3. What is the maximum age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Medical Officer (General) post is 37 years. However, age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per government rules.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are available for the Medical Officer (General) post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 41 vacancies for the Medical Officer (General) post in this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Medical Officer (General) position?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will be appointed as per Pay Level 16 of the ROPA - 2019.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for UR/EWS/OBC candidates?
faq.answer: The application fee for UR, EWS, and OBC candidates is Rs. 200, which includes Rs. 150 as application fee and Rs. 50 as processing charges.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for SC/ST/PWD candidates?
faq.answer: SC, ST, and PWD candidates are required to pay Rs. 50, which is the processing charge only.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for the Medical Officer (General) recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process will involve a Written Examination followed by a Personality Test.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any experience required for this post?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates need to have at least 01 year of experience in the practice of medicine or working experience in the field of Public Health.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and apply online?
faq.answer: The official notification and the online application link will be available on the official website of the West Bengal Municipal Service Commission (WBMSC). Links are provided in the 'Important Links' section of this article.
